sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Standard Liège have had a mixed run in their last 5 matches, securing 1 win, 1 draw, and 3 losses, with a recent 1-0 defeat to RSC Anderlecht. Royal Antwerp FC are also struggling, winless in their last 5 matches (2 draws, 3 losses), with their latest result being a 1-1 draw against Cercle Brugge. Both teams are desperate for points, sitting close in the league table at 12th and 13th respectively.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two encounters between these sides, Royal Antwerp FC have dominated, with a 3-0 victory in October 2024 and a 0-0 draw in March 2025. Historically, Antwerp have been unbeaten in their last 6 matches against Standard Liège, winning 4 of those, which gives them a psychological edge.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Standard Liège have struggled offensively, scoring only 9 goals this season (13th in the league), while their defense has been average, conceding 13 goals. Royal Antwerp FC have a slightly better attack with 10 goals but a similar defensive record (12 conceded). Both teams lack firepower, suggesting a low-scoring affair.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: For Standard Liège, Marco Ilaimaharitra (2 goals, 6.95 avg. rating) could be crucial in midfield, while Royal Antwerp FC will rely on Vincent Janssen (2 goals, 6.99 avg. rating), who has a strong record against Liège with 5 goals in past matchups. Their contributions could tilt the balance.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given the Sofascore probabilities (32% home win, 36% draw, 32% away win) and recent form, a draw seems the most likely outcome. Antwerp's historical dominance is tempered by their current winless streak, while Standard Liège's home advantage at Maurice Dufrasne might not be enough for a win. Expect a cautious, low-scoring game.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, Standard Liège holds a narrow lead over Royal Antwerp FC with a score of 1-0. The home side struck early through Rafiki Saïd, who found the net in the very first minute. Despite being down to ten men after a red card to Adnane Abid, Standard Liège has managed to maintain their advantage. Royal Antwerp FC, on the other hand, has dominated possession but struggled to create clear-cut chances, failing to register a single shot on target in the first half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance
halftimeSections.text: Standard Liège has shown resilience despite their numerical disadvantage. Their defense has been relatively solid, while their attacking intent was evident in the early stages. Royal Antwerp FC has controlled the ball for the majority of the half but has been unable to convert their possession into meaningful opportunities. Their lack of accuracy in the final third and inability to test the opposition goalkeeper is a significant concern heading into the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum
halftimeSections.text: The momentum in attack has seen several shifts throughout the first half. Standard Liège started with a burst of energy, dominating the early minutes with consistent pressure. As the half progressed, Royal Antwerp FC gained ground with prolonged spells of attacking intent, particularly in the middle of the half, pushing forward with purpose. However, Standard Liège regained control towards the latter stages, showing flashes of danger despite being a man down.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players
halftimeSections.text: For Standard Liège, Rafiki Saïd has been the standout performer with his early goal, proving to be a constant threat. Tobias Mohr has also been influential with his creative play in midfield. On the Royal Antwerp FC side, Farouck Adekami has shown promise in attack, while Mauricio Benitez has contributed well in the middle of the park. Antwerp will need their forwards, particularly Vincent Janssen, to step up if they are to turn things around.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half outlook
halftimeSections.text: The second half promises to be intriguing, with Standard Liège likely to focus on defending their lead while looking for counter-attacking opportunities. Their reduced numbers might force a more conservative approach. Royal Antwerp FC, with their possession dominance, will push hard for an equalizer and potentially more. They are expected to capitalize on their extra man and increase the tempo to break down Standard Liège's defense.
